 Session 56 closed historical contamination cleanup and shipped BUG 4

 (F8 diocesan research playbook, commit 06f8386). 2 bugs remain.

 Session 57 picks up with:

 1. PRIORITY 1 — BUG 1: F4 funding scanner query redesign

 (memory/project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md). Enter plan mode,

 self-pressure-test hard before presenting (see

 feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md — every plan is a one-shot).

 Not a prompt fix. Queries need site filters targeting *.k12.*.us

 domains + state DOE subdomains. Build a local Serper-replay

 diagnostic harness so iteration doesn't need Railway redeploys.

 Kill switch ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN currently False in

 tools/signal_processor.py.

 2. PRIORITY 2 — BUG 2: F5 CSTA strategic decision

 (memory/project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md). ANSWER THE

 STRATEGIC QUESTION FIRST before any code: standalone scanner or

 F2-enrichment layer? Kill switch ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN currently False.

 Then in order: Session 52 Stages 6-8 carryover, diocesan approval

 drip via /prospect_approve (16 Catholic dioceses pending in queue

 since Session 56 will auto-activate the playbook — expect name-only

 yield), sequence_builder private_school_network branch write-up.

 Operator capabilities still live from Sessions 55-56:

 - Telethon bridge (scripts/tg_send.py + tg_recent.py) — drive Scout

 end-to-end from Claude Code

 - screencapture -x + Read — screenshot Steven's Mac for visual

 debugging

 - Railway log API via RAILWAY_TOKEN in .env (project-scoped; logs

 endpoint works, deployments endpoint returns 403 — known)

 Kill switch state:

 - ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN=True (F2)

 - ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN=False (F4, BUG 1)

 - ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN=False (F5, BUG 2)

 - ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY=False (F8 Serper ad-hoc discovery;

 note this does NOT gate /prospect_private_networks seed queueing,

 which was used successfully in Session 56)

 - ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER=True (BUG 5)

 - ENABLE_DIOCESAN_PLAYBOOK=True (BUG 4, Session 56)

 Rules established this session (full detail in CLAUDE.md Session 56

 lessons block and memory/feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md):

 - Every plan is a one-shot. Self-pressure-test hard; never frame a

 plan around expecting Steven to run pressure-test rounds.

 - Verify foundational assumptions empirically BEFORE writing plans —

 walk the pipeline end-to-end and confirm each load-bearing

 assumption is actually true in production.

 - Serper snippets flow into raw_pages via _add_raw_from_serper. For

 JS-rendered sites (Liferay/React/Drupal-SPA diocesan CMS,

 charter networks, modern public district redesigns), search layers

 carry yield without direct HTTP fetch — don't invest in L6 seed

 URL enumeration.

 - Single source of truth helpers prevent filter leaks. When a

 matching rule needs a per-context override, refactor ALL call

 sites behind a single helper first, then add the override.

 - Scout slash command naming is inconsistent — e.g.

 /prospect_private_networks NOT /queue_private_school_networks for

 the queue_private_school_networks() function. Always reference

 agent/main.py handler block before firing non-obvious slash

 commands.

 · thought for 22s)

 ⏺ Second round destroys more of the bug memory's prescription. Let me summarize findings before entering plan mode, then write the plan.
 Empirical findings:

 1. site:*.k12.il.us / site:isbe.net = near-zero results. Districts don't self-announce grant receipts on their own sites consistently; state DOE direct-site filters are too narrow. The bug memory's primary
 prescription is wrong.
 2. What actually works (ranked):

 - PA : "awarded" "computer science" grant Pennsylvania "school district" 2025 → real hits: Berwick Area SD + PAsmart, School District of Philadelphia $450K, Saucon Valley $75K STEM, PA 2025-26 Grant Book PDF

 - OH : "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded → 3 real district-level hits (Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0)

 - IL : "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant recipients 2025 → ISBE Weekly Message PDF (real signal)

 - Fed : site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded 2025 Illinois → ed.gov EIR "Community Unit School District 60, $694,176" — a real district-level federal award

 3. Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile — my "Advancing Computer Science Education" TX guess returned 1 noise hit. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS has only 5 of 13 territory states covered and some entries are
 unverified.

 4. Truncation is secondary. With 5 queries × 13 states the real issue is low-signal corpus, not too much signal. Per-state chunked Claude calls still fix the 14K cap cleanly.

 Now entering plan mode. This plan is a one-shot — the foundational assumption has been verified against empirical Serper responses, every commit has a rollback, and call sites are enumerated.

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 BUG 1 — F4 CS Funding Scanner Query Redesign

 Session 57, 2026-04-11. One-shot plan.

 Context

 tools/signal_processor.py:scan_cs_funding_awards (line 3152) has returned zero extractions on every run since F4 shipped in Session 49. Session 53's spot-check confirmed the Serper leg fires (456 articles

 across 13 states) but Claude Haiku correctly extracts nothing because the search corpus is starving — the current query templates match higher-ed grants, teacher-level awards, student scholarships, legislative

 text, and bill PDFs, not K-12 district-level funding announcements. ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN has been off since Session 53 and /signal_funding is currently manual-only (no daily schedule). Project memory is

 project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md ; the memory's "four root causes" list was written before empirical verification.

 Foundational assumption re-verified against live Serper (2026-04-11 probe — see "Empirical Findings" below). The bug memory's primary prescription ("use *.k12.*.us + state DOE subdomain site filters") is

 partially wrong . I ran 14 live probes before writing this plan. Site-filter queries like site:k12.il.us , site:*.k12.il.us , site:isbe.net return 0-1 results because (a) K-12 districts don't self-announce grant

 receipts on their own public pages consistently, and (b) state-DOE direct-site filters over-restrict against actually-indexed content that lives in weekly-message PDFs and report attachments. The winning query

 patterns are (1) broad natural-language "awarded" + "computer science" + <State> + "school district" , (2) state-DOE proper-name (quoted, no site filter), and (3) program-name queries where the program name is

 verified correct.

 This plan redesigns the query corpus around what empirically works, fixes the secondary truncation issue via per-state chunking, tightens the extraction prompt, ships a Serper-replay diagnostic harness so

 iteration doesn't need Railway redeploys, and gates re-enabling behind a manual validation pass — same bar F9 held to.

 Empirical findings (2026-04-11 Serper probes — this is the load-bearing data)

 Fourteen live Serper queries, tbs: qdr:y , each against 5-10 organic results.

 What does NOT work:

 - site:k12.il.us "computer science" grant award → 0 results

 - site:*.k12.il.us "awarded" "computer science" → 1 result (student award, not grant)

 - site:isbe.net "computer science" grant recipients → 0 results

 - site:k12dive.com "computer science" grant awarded district 2025 → 5 commentary articles, 0 recipient news

 - site:ed.gov "school district" "computer science" grant recipient → 7 results, all methodology/FIPSE PDFs, 0 recipient lists ranked

 - site:nsf.gov "computer science" "school district" award → 5 results, all research papers / budget requests

 What DOES work:

 - "awarded" "computer science" grant Pennsylvania "school district" 2025 → real district hits : Berwick Area School District (PAsmart Grant), School District of Philadelphia ($450K), Saucon Valley SD ($75K

 STEM), PA 2025-26 Grant Book PDF. 5 of 6 usable.

 - "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded → Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0 Grant (3 mentions across news-herald, yahoo, ed.ohio.gov policy). 3 of 5 usable.

 - "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant recipients 2025 (NO site filter, DOE NAME quoted) → ISBE Weekly Message PDF with CS grant section, supported-by-ISBE facebook district references.

 Usable signal in top 3.

 - site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded 2025 Illinois → ed.gov EIR page with snippet containing "Community Unit School District 60, Computer Science and Engineering and Design STEM

 Program, $694,176" — a real district + amount + purpose in the snippet itself. 1 high-value hit. (Generic .gov filter, not site:ed.gov .)

 - Existing "STEM grant" awarded school district "<State>" (already in code) — the PA probe confirms this pattern returns real district press releases. Keep it.

 What is fragile:

 - Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile: my probe with "Advancing Computer Science Education" Texas TEA awarded districts 2025 returned 1 noise hit. The TX entry in STATE_CS_PROGRAMS ("TEA Advancing

 CS") is unverified. Program names must be VERIFIED via a harness probe before being added to the query list — wrong names burn Serper budget and add zero signal. Non-program queries must work standalone so

 Type A/C/F can carry the scan on states where no program is verified.

 Truncation:

 - Current: combined_text = combined_text[:14000] (line 3242). IL alone is 12,486 chars (fits). 13-state production = ~137K (keeps first ~10%). Truncation is real at production scale but secondary to the

 corpus-starvation issue. Fix via per-state chunked Claude calls — same pattern as current per-state Serper loop. Per-state payloads are ~10K chars each, well under Haiku's 200K context.

 Decisions

 1. Query types, not flat list. Redesign around 4 query TYPES per state. Types A, C, F are program-agnostic and always run. Types B run only when STATE_CS_PROGRAMS[state] has verified program names.

 2. No *.k12.*.us site filters. Empirically dead. Waste of Serper budget.

 3. No site:isbe.net / site:ed.gov direct-DOE filters. Same reason.

 4. Per-state chunked Claude extraction. One Haiku call per state, ≤10K chars each. 13 states × ~$0.002 = ~$0.026 per scan. Trivial vs the $0.10 the docstring estimated.

 5. Expand STATE_CS_PROGRAMS conservatively. Only add entries I can verify via the harness. Unverified entries stay commented out with # UNVERIFIED and are NOT used for Type B generation. Ship with IL/PA/OH

 verified (3 of 5 current); verify or drop TX/MA during implementation; leave CA/MI/CT/OK/IN/NV/TN/NE program-name-free and rely on Types A/C/F.

 6. Tighten Haiku prompt. Require "real LEA name — no program descriptions, no aggregate phrases like '23 schools', no multi-entity 'recipients' rollups". Drop redundant Awards >12 months old exclude ( tbs:

 qdr:y already enforces it).

 7. Serper-replay diagnostic harness shipped as first commit, before any scanner code changes. Snapshot Serper to JSON, replay extraction offline, diff against hand-verified oracle. Steven or I can iterate

 prompt without burning Railway redeploys or Serper credits.

 8. Manual validation gate + separate enable commit. Even after code lands, ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN stays False . A separate 4th commit flips it — conditional on ≥60% of HIGH-confidence extractions in the harness

 oracle being real K-12 LEA awards (same F9 bar).

 9. No auto-queue behavior changes. Current HIGH→queue / MEDIUM+LOW→signals routing stays. The only question under test is extraction yield , not routing .

 What this plan deliberately does NOT do

 - No new scanner, no new signal type. Keep signal_type="cs_funding_award" , strategy="cs_funding_recipient" . The routing, dedup, and priority-scoring code already works — only the Serper leg + Claude leg

 change.

 - No F1/F2/F5/F9 changes. Strictly isolated to F4.

 - No schema/column additions to Signals tab or Prospecting Queue. sheets_writer.write_signals and district_prospector.add_district signatures unchanged.

 - No scheduled-scan wiring. /signal_funding stays manual-only. Daily schedule addition is a separate Session-58+ decision conditional on validation gate passing.

 - No historical cleanup. F4 has never produced contamination (it produced 0). There's nothing to clean.

 - No program-name auto-discovery. Curating STATE_CS_PROGRAMS stays manual — the harness surfaces "which states have a known-good program name" but doesn't auto-add.

 - No news-site allowlist. Types A/C/F let local news rank naturally. Maintaining a site:news-X.com OR site:news-Y.com list would rot fast and is unnecessary because "awarded" + district keyword already pulls

 news naturally (PA probe proves this).

 - No ed.gov EIR historical dump. Could one-shot scrape the EIR award list to seed historical CS recipients, but that's a different feature — out of scope.

 Architecture

 No new modules, no new classes, no main.py changes.

 Three files touched across four commits:

 1. scripts/f4_serper_replay.py — NEW diagnostic harness (read-only, no deploy impact)

 2. tools/signal_processor.py — query redesign + per-state chunking + prompt tightening + STATE_CS_PROGRAMS expansion

 3. tools/signal_processor.py — separate 1-line commit flipping ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN

 The /signal_funding handler at agent/main.py:3314 is unchanged; it already forwards states=None, progress_callback=None correctly.

 Query types (generator pseudocode)

 def _f4_build_queries(state_abbr: str , state_name: str ) -> list [ str ]:

 """Generate the F4 query list for one state. Types A, C, F are always

 emitted. Type B only when a verified program name exists."""

 queries = [

 # Type A — natural-language recipient announcement (broadest, highest-yield)

 f'"awarded" "computer science" grant "{state_name}" "school district"' ,

 # Type A2 — alt verb

 f'"computer science" grant "selected" OR "recipients" "school district" "{state_name}"' ,

 # Type C — state DOE by NAME (no site filter, name quoted)

 f'"{state_name} State Board of Education" "computer science" grant' ,

 f'"{state_name} Department of Education" "computer science" OR "STEM" grant awarded' ,

 # Type F — retain existing STEM-grant query (PA probe confirms it works)

 f'"STEM grant" awarded school district "{state_name}"' ,

 # Type G — generic .gov filter (federal EIR + state .gov PDFs)

 f'site:.gov "{state_name}" "school district" "computer science" grant recipient' ,

 ]

 # Type B — program-name queries, only when verified

 for program_name in STATE_CS_PROGRAMS.get(state_abbr, []):

 queries.append( f'"{program_name}" awarded "{state_name}" district' )

 queries.append( f'"{program_name}" recipients "{state_name}"' )

 return queries

 Per-state query count: 6 baseline + 2 per verified program = 6-10. For 13 states with 3 programs verified (IL/PA/OH), total = 13×6 + 3×2 = 84 queries per scan vs current 65. Budget delta: +19 Serper calls ×

 $0.001 = ~$0.02/scan. Negligible.

 Per-state chunked extraction

 Replace the global combined_text[:14000] block with a per-state loop. Each state builds its own Haiku payload from its own snippets, calls client.messages.create once, appends the JSON list to a master items

 list. The downstream routing loop (lines 3322-3415) operates on the merged list and is unchanged.

 items = []

 for state_abbr in scan_states:

 state_snippets = [s for s in all_snippets if s[ "searched_state" ] == state_abbr]

 if not state_snippets:

 continue

 combined_text = ""

 for s in state_snippets:

 combined_text += f"\n[{s['searched_state']}] {s['title']}\n{s['snippet']}\nURL: {s['url']}\n"

 # Per-state cap still cuts pathological cases (one state with 80+ hits)

 combined_text = combined_text[: 20000 ]

 state_items = _f4_extract_items(client, combined_text, state_abbr, logger)

 items.extend(state_items)

 _f4_extract_items wraps the existing prompt + JSON-array parsing + JSONDecodeError logging into a helper, returns list[dict] (empty on any failure). One helper, one call site, keeps the existing routing loop

 untouched.

 Current prompt lines 3250-3282. Change in two places:

 1. Tighter district definition — add before EXCLUDE: block:

 DISTRICT NAME RULES:

 - Must be a specific named Local Education Agency (LEA) — the exact

 legal name of one K-12 school district.

 - REJECT program descriptions (e.g. "23 west central Illinois schools")

 - REJECT aggregate phrases (e.g. "CS grant recipients", "participating districts")

 - REJECT charter networks, CMOs, ESCs, BOCES unless the snippet clearly

 names a single LEA as the direct recipient

 - Single school without district context → confidence=LOW, mark for review

 2. Drop redundant exclude : Delete - Awards >12 months old line (line 3277). qdr:y enforces it at the Serper leg.

 STATE_CS_PROGRAMS expansion

 Current (line 2978):

 STATE_CS_PROGRAMS = {

 "TX" : [ "TEA Advancing CS" ],

 "IL" : [ "CS Equity Grant" ],

 "MA" : [ "Innovation Pathways CS" ],

 "PA" : [ "PAsmart" ],

 "OH" : [ "Computer Science Promise" ],

 }

 After verification via harness Step 2 (see Verification section), commit the following. Any unverified entries stay commented out with # UNVERIFIED <date> and are NOT included in the dict that

 _f4_build_queries reads.

 STATE_CS_PROGRAMS = {

 "IL" : [ "CS Equity Grant" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 "PA" : [ "PAsmart" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 "OH" : [ "Computer Science Promise" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 # "TX": ["TEA Advancing CS"], # UNVERIFIED 2026-04-11 — only 1 noise hit

 # "MA": ["Innovation Pathways CS"], # UNVERIFIED — re-probe during implementation

 # Other territory states: no known program; Types A/C/F/G carry these.

 }

 If TX or MA verify during implementation, add them; if they don't, keep them commented. Do not ship unverified program names into production.

 Commit A — Serper-replay diagnostic harness (NO scanner changes)

 New file: scripts/f4_serper_replay.py

 Modes:

 - --snapshot <state> — runs the new query list for one state against live Serper, saves scripts/f4_corpus_<state>_<YYYYMMDD>.json

 - --snapshot-all — same for all 13 territory states (sequential, honors the existing 0.5s sleep between calls)

 - --replay <snapshot_path> — loads a snapshot, runs the new Haiku prompt against it, prints extracted items + counts

 - --diff <old_snapshot> <new_snapshot> — prints URL-level delta between two corpora (useful for comparing old vs new query design)

 - --gate <oracle.json> — loads a hand-verified oracle, runs Haiku against each oracle state's snapshot, computes HIGH-confidence precision (reals / total_high). Prints PASS if ≥60%, FAIL otherwise.

 Harness uses the real _f4_build_queries and _f4_extract_items helpers from tools.signal_processor so divergence between harness and production is impossible. (This is the same single-source-of-truth rule that

 Commit C of BUG 4 used.)

 Oracle: scripts/f4_oracle.json , hand-curated during the validation gate step. Format mirrors bug5_oracle_*.json — rows with {district, state, expected_confidence, is_real_lea_award: bool, notes} . 20-30 rows

 across 3-5 states is enough.

 No production impact. Commit A is pure diagnostic tooling. Rollback = revert the commit; nothing else references the harness.

 Commit B — query redesign + per-state chunking + prompt tightening

 tools/signal_processor.py :

 1. Line 2978 — STATE_CS_PROGRAMS dict: replace with the verified subset (3 entries), leave unverified as commented-out. Add # verified <date> per entry.

 2. Line 3152 — inside scan_cs_funding_awards :

 - Replace the inline 5-query list (lines 3194-3205) with a call to the new _f4_build_queries(state_abbr, state_name) helper.

 - Replace the global combined_text[:14000] (lines 3239-3242) + single Haiku call (lines 3247-3290) with a per-state loop calling _f4_extract_items . Merge results into a master items list. Downstream routing

 loop (lines 3322-3415) stays untouched.

 3. New helpers (private, module-level, next to STATE_CS_PROGRAMS ):

 def _f4_build_queries(state_abbr: str , state_name: str ) -> list [ str ]: ...

 def _f4_extract_items(client, combined_text: str , state_abbr: str , logger) -> list [ dict ]: ...

 3. Both are private helpers, callable from scan_cs_funding_awards AND from the harness. Zero duplication.

 4. Prompt inside _f4_extract_items :

 - Add DISTRICT NAME RULES block before EXCLUDE:

 - Delete - Awards >12 months old line

 - Bump max_tokens=2500 → max_tokens=3000 (more headroom for multi-state chunks; harmless if unused)

 5. Logging : per-state Haiku call logs F4 {state_abbr}: {len(state_snippets)} snippets → {len(state_items)} extracted . Operator visibility without needing the harness.

 Behavior while ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN=False : identical to current (short-circuit returns empty result). Commit B is dead code in production until Commit D.

 Rollback: git revert — single-file revert, no cross-module coupling.

 Commit C — harness oracle + validation gate pass

 New file: scripts/f4_oracle.json — 20-30 rows hand-verified by Steven (or me with Steven reviewing) against the snapshots produced by the harness. Each row: {district, state, source_url, expected_confidence,

 is_real_lea_award, notes} .

 Run harness gate:

 python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --snapshot-all

 python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --gate scripts/f4_oracle.json

 Pass criterion (exit code 0): of all HIGH-confidence extractions across the 13-state snapshot, ≥60% match a real K-12 LEA funding award in the oracle (or pass manual spot-check if not pre-labeled in the

 oracle). False positives at MEDIUM/LOW are acceptable — those only go to the Signals tab for human review.

 If gate fails:

 - Iterate on _f4_build_queries or prompt in a follow-up commit

 - Rerun --snapshot-all + --gate

 - No Railway impact because scanner is still disabled

 Commit contents: the oracle JSON file + a brief scripts/f4_oracle_README.md documenting the gate criterion and how to regenerate. Zero code changes to production paths.

 Commit D — one-line enable flip

 After Commit C's gate passes and Steven signs off on the oracle's HIGH-confidence roster:

 tools/signal_processor.py line 1128:

 -ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN = False # F4: DISABLED Session 53 — wrong query corpus since Session 49 (project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md)

 +ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN = True # F4: Re-enabled Session 57 after query redesign (BUG 1) — see scripts/f4_serper_replay.py gate

 Rollback: one-line flip back to False . No revert needed, no sheet cleanup, no coupling.

 Verification

 Pre-flight (runs after each of Commits A and B, before push)

 source .venv/bin/activate

 # 1. Scanner still importable, function signature unchanged

 assert ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN is False, 'kill switch must stay off until Commit D'

 "

 Live Serper corpus snapshot (Commit A deliverable)

 # Expect: 13 JSON files, one per territory state, each with title/snippet/url/searched_state per hit

 Offline replay + gate (Commit C deliverable)

 # Expect: extracted JSON list, HIGH-confidence count, at least Berwick Area School District + Philadelphia SD hits

 Live end-to-end (Commit D post-deploy)

 # Wait for bot response in Telegram (~2-3 min for 13-state Serper loop + 13 Haiku calls)

 # Expect: "💵 CS Funding Scan — Raw extracted: 15+, Signals written: 8+"

 python3 scripts/tg_recent.py -- limit 20

 Railway log inspection for extraction yield per state:

 # Expect lines like: F4 IL: 42 snippets → 3 extracted; F4 PA: 51 snippets → 4 extracted

 Sheet verification:

 python3 -c "

 import os, json

 from pathlib import Path

 for ln in Path('.env').read_text().splitlines():

 if '=' in ln and not ln.startswith('#'):

 k,_,v = ln.partition('=')

 os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip(\"'\\\"\"))

 from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(json.loads(os.environ['GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON']), scopes=['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets.readonly'])

 svc = build('sheets','v4',credentials=creds)

 v = svc.spreadsheets().values().get(spreadsheetId=os.environ['GOOGLE_SHEETS_ID'], range=\"Signals!A1:R200\").execute().get('values',[])

 hdr = v[0]

 rows = [dict(zip(hdr, r)) for r in v[1:] if len(r) >= 5 and r[4] == 'cs_funding_award']

 print(f'{len(rows)} cs_funding_award rows in Signals tab')

 for r in rows[-10:]:

 print(f\" {r.get('Date','')} {r.get('District','')} ({r.get('State','')}) — {r.get('Dollar Amount','')} — {r.get('Headline','')[:80]}\")

 "

 Pass criterion for Commit D live run: ≥5 signals written total across 13 states OR at least 1 HIGH-confidence auto-queue. Zero is the prior baseline and would mean the gate was mis-calibrated. Failure → flip

 kill switch back to False , diagnose from the snapshot.

 Risks that aren't obvious

 1. Harness divergence from production. Mitigated by importing _f4_build_queries and _f4_extract_items directly from tools.signal_processor — the harness has no copy of those functions.

 2. Haiku multi-state cost surge. 13 calls vs 1 is ~13× Haiku token cost per scan. Current docstring says $0.10/scan; new ~$0.03-0.05 based on smaller per-state payloads (shorter prompts fit more state-specific

 context without waste). Net: comparable or slightly cheaper.

 3. Oracle freshness rot. Oracle is built from one --snapshot-all run. Six months later the qdr:y window has rotated and the oracle is stale. Mitigation: oracle README documents that it's a point-in-time

 artifact and should be regenerated when doing a re-verification pass. Not a recurring cost — only regenerate when fixing another bug.

 4. State-name collision in Type C queries. "Indiana State Board of Education" — some states have multiple boards (e.g., Ohio has ODE + Ohio State Board). Not blocking: Type A+F+G carry the state, and Type C

 just adds supplemental signal. Probe during harness snapshot will surface any state where Type C returns 0 — document it but don't block.

 5. Rate-limit / 429s from Serper during --snapshot-all . Existing 0.5s sleep is preserved. 84 queries × 0.5s = 42s minimum per snapshot. Fine for a manual harness invocation.

 6. Prompt tightening false-negatives. New "must be a specific named LEA" rule might reject legitimate edge cases like "participating districts" where the participant list is in a linked PDF not in the snippet.

 7. Session 53 diagnostic artifacts might exist but don't. I checked scripts/ — no pre-existing F4 diagnostic scripts. The "Session 53 diagnostic" referenced in the bug memory was an inline local replay that

 wasn't committed.

 ├─────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┤

 │ scripts/f4_oracle.json │ — │ — │ ✓ NEW │ — │

 ├─────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┤

 │ tools/signal_processor.py │ — │ ✓ query redesign + per-state chunking + prompt + STATE_CS_PROGRAMS │ — │ ✓ 1-line kill-switch flip │

 ├─────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┤

 │ agent/main.py │ — │ — │ — │ — │

 └─────────────────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┘

 Zero agent/main.py changes. Zero schema changes. Zero CLAUDE.md changes during the fix sprint (CLAUDE.md gets the Session 57 update at end-of-session, same pattern as every other session).

 Out of scope (deliberate)

 - Scheduled F4 daily scan (currently manual via /signal_funding )

 - ed.gov EIR historical recipient seed scrape

 - NCES CCD grant-data integration

 - F5 CSTA scanner (BUG 2, Priority 2 — separate plan)

 - Charter CMO / CTE center / private school / compliance carryover (Session 52 Stages 6-8)

 - Program-name auto-discovery via harness (manual curation only)

 - CA-specific program-name research (15 probes would burn budget with low yield confidence)

 Post-plan steps for next session

 After Commit D passes validation and ships:

 1. Monitor /signal_funding output over 2-3 daily manual runs

 2. Spot-check HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows in Prospecting Queue against real district websites

 3. If spot-check confirms ≥60% precision, add F4 to the scheduler ( agent/scheduler.py — separate commit, separate session)

 4. Move to BUG 2 (F5 CSTA scanner) — answer strategic question (standalone vs F2-enrichment) first, per Priority 2 instructions

 Rollback ladder

 1. Commit A (harness): git revert — zero production impact either way

 2. Commit B (query redesign): git revert OR leave in place with ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN=False (already dead code)

 3. Commit C (oracle): git revert — diagnostic artifact only

 4. Commit D (enable): one-line flip True → False , push, done. No sheet cleanup — signals and queued rows written by a broken run are a separate cleanup task if the gate was wrong. Since the scanner has

 produced 0 rows historically, the first bad run is unlikely to be large.

 Hard-stop: if at any commit the pre-flight checks fail, halt, diagnose, fix before pushing. Never push a failing pre-flight.

 [<u[>1u[>4;2m

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it honestly — keep what's
 genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back something I can execute
 with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 Session 56 closed historical contamination cleanup and shipped BUG 4

 (F8 diocesan research playbook, commit 06f8386). 2 bugs remain.

 Session 57 picks up with:

 1. PRIORITY 1 — BUG 1: F4 funding scanner query redesign

 (memory/project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md). Enter plan mode,

 self-pressure-test hard before presenting (see

 feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md — every plan is a one-shot).

 Not a prompt fix. Queries need site filters targeting *.k12.*.us

 domains + state DOE subdomains. Build a local Serper-replay

 diagnostic harness so iteration doesn't need Railway redeploys.

 Kill switch ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN currently False in

 tools/signal_processor.py.

 2. PRIORITY 2 — BUG 2: F5 CSTA strategic decision

 (memory/project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md). ANSWER THE

 STRATEGIC QUESTION FIRST before any code: standalone scanner or

 F2-enrichment layer? Kill switch ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN currently False.

 Then in order: Session 52 Stages 6-8 carryover, diocesan approval

 drip via /prospect_approve (16 Catholic dioceses pending in queue

 since Session 56 will auto-activate the playbook — expect name-only

 yield), sequence_builder private_school_network branch write-up.

 Operator capabilities still live from Sessions 55-56:

 - Telethon bridge (scripts/tg_send.py + tg_recent.py) — drive Scout

 end-to-end from Claude Code

 - screencapture -x + Read — screenshot Steven's Mac for visual

 debugging

 - Railway log API via RAILWAY_TOKEN in .env (project-scoped; logs

 endpoint works, deployments endpoint returns 403 — known)

 Kill switch state:

 - ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN=True (F2)

 - ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN=False (F4, BUG 1)

 - ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN=False (F5, BUG 2)

 - ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY=False (F8 Serper ad-hoc discovery;

 note this does NOT gate /prospect_private_networks seed queueing,

 which was used successfully in Session 56)

 - ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER=True (BUG 5)

 - ENABLE_DIOCESAN_PLAYBOOK=True (BUG 4, Session 56)

 Rules established this session (full detail in CLAUDE.md Session 56

 lessons block and memory/feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md):

 - Every plan is a one-shot. Self-pressure-test hard; never frame a

 plan around expecting Steven to run pressure-test rounds.

 - Verify foundational assumptions empirically BEFORE writing plans —

 walk the pipeline end-to-end and confirm each load-bearing

 assumption is actually true in production.

 - Serper snippets flow into raw_pages via _add_raw_from_serper. For

 JS-rendered sites (Liferay/React/Drupal-SPA diocesan CMS,

 charter networks, modern public district redesigns), search layers

 carry yield without direct HTTP fetch — don't invest in L6 seed

 URL enumeration.

 - Single source of truth helpers prevent filter leaks. When a

 matching rule needs a per-context override, refactor ALL call

 sites behind a single helper first, then add the override.

 - Scout slash command naming is inconsistent — e.g.

 /prospect_private_networks NOT /queue_private_school_networks for

 the queue_private_school_networks() function. Always reference

 agent/main.py handler block before firing non-obvious slash

 commands.

 ⏺ I'll start by reading the Session 56 plan, the F4 bug memory, SCOUT_PLAN.md, and the F4 scanner code in parallel. The Vercel verification/workflow skills don't apply here — this is a Python/Railway Scout

 import os, json, httpx…)

 Q: "computer science" OR "CS" grant awarded school district "Illinois"

 ⏺ Critical empirical finding — the bug memory's prescription is partially WRONG. Let me verify one more angle before I write the plan.

 ⏺ Bash (source .venv/bin/activate 2>/dev/null && python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, httpx…)

 ⎿   == FED EIR recipients == Q: site:ed.gov "school district" "computer science" grant recipient 2024 OR 2025

 1. Education Innovation and Research | U.S. Department of Education || http://www.ed.gov/grants-and-programs/grants-special-populations/grants-economically-disad

 2. [PDF] Fund for the Improvement of Postsecondary Education (FIPSE ... || https://www.ed.gov/media/document/frequently-asked-questions-fy-2025-fipse-sp-competition-

 … +26 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  (timeout 1m)

 ⏺ Second round destroys more of the bug memory's prescription. Let me summarize findings before entering plan mode, then write the plan.

 Empirical findings:

 1. site:*.k12.il.us / site:isbe.net = near-zero results. Districts don't self-announce grant receipts on their own sites consistently; state DOE direct-site filters are too narrow. The bug memory's primary

 prescription is wrong.

 2. What actually works (ranked):

 - PA : "awarded" "computer science" grant Pennsylvania "school district" 2025 → real hits: Berwick Area SD + PAsmart, School District of Philadelphia $450K, Saucon Valley $75K STEM, PA 2025-26 Grant Book PDF

 - OH : "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded → 3 real district-level hits (Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0)

 - IL : "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant recipients 2025 → ISBE Weekly Message PDF (real signal)

 - Fed : site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded 2025 Illinois → ed.gov EIR "Community Unit School District 60, $694,176" — a real district-level federal award

 3. Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile — my "Advancing Computer Science Education" TX guess returned 1 noise hit. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS has only 5 of 13 territory states covered and some entries are

 unverified.

 4. Truncation is secondary. With 5 queries × 13 states the real issue is low-signal corpus, not too much signal. Per-state chunked Claude calls still fix the 14K cap cleanly.

 Now entering plan mode. This plan is a one-shot — the foundational assumption has been verified against empirical Serper responses, every commit has a rollback, and call sites are enumerated.

 ⏺ Entered plan mode

 Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

 per-state chunking precedent, and /signal_funding command existence.

 ⏺ Running the ruthless pressure test. Holding the full plan in my head first, then I'll interrogate foundation, stress-test every layer, fix what's wrong, and rewrite from scratch.

 What's genuinely strong (keep):

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Empirical foundation: 14 live Serper probes disproved the bug memory's site:*.k12.*.us prescription. That section is load-bearing truth.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Per-state chunked Haiku calls — clean, cheap, gives per-state log visibility.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Harness-first sequencing: build diagnostic tooling before touching production code.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Single source-of-truth helpers ( _f4_build_queries , _f4_extract_items ) callable from both harness and production.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Manual validation gate + separate enable commit. Correct discipline.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Empirical foundation: 14 live Serper probes disproved the bug memory's site:*.k12.*.us prescription. That section is load-bearing truth.
 Per-state ch ked Haiku calls — cl an, ch ap, gives per-state log visibility.
 Ha ness-first s quencing: build diag ostic tool ng before touching production code.
 Single sou ce-of-truth helpers ( _f4_build_queries , _f4_extract_items ) callable from both harness and production.
 Ma ual validation ga e + separate enable commit. Correct discipline.
 Rollback a der + kill-switch pr s rvation.
 What's actually wrong:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 1. STATE_DOE_NAMES missing — Type C queries are broken for ~half the territory. I wrote "<State> State Board of Education" as the template. Texas grants come from TEA (Texas Education Agency), not a "State
 Board." Ohio = ODE. California = CDE. Connecticut = CSDE. My template would miss the actual grant-issuing body for at least TX, CT, MA, and probably IN/NV. This is a silent correctness bug hiding in my
 "solution." Fix: ship a hand-curated STATE_DOE_NAMES constant covering all 13 territory states with verified proper names.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 2. Type A2 is unverified speculation. "computer science" grant "selected" OR "recipients" — I invented this, didn't probe it. Plans should not ship queries I haven't empirically tested. Cut it.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 3. Oracle bootstrap is underspecified. "Hand-curated during validation gate step" is hand-waving. Reality: somebody has to click 20-30 URLs and label them. If that's Steven, I'm wasting his time. If that's me,
 I need to describe how (httpx fetch + human review of snippets). Make explicit: I build the initial oracle via URL fetch + label proposal, Steven reviews the labels, commit the final JSON.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 4. Gate failure has no exit criterion. "Iterate on queries/prompt" is infinite. A well-run fix has a max-iteration cap. If 3 harness rounds don't get HIGH precision ≥60%, escalate to Alternative C (direct PDF
 ingestion of state-DOE grant-report PDFs + federal EIR tables). Session 57 may not close BUG 1 — that has to be OK, stated up front.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 5. Per-state JSON parse failure semantics not explicit. Current code catches JSONDecodeError at global scope and kills the whole scan on one bad response. In a 13-state loop, one bad state must not kill the
 other 12. _f4_extract_items must catch internally and return [] for the failing state. I hinted at it but didn't state it.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 6. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS aspirations waste plan bandwidth. I said "verify TX/MA during implementation." TX has no analog to PAsmart — TEA doesn't run a named district grant program comparable to Illinois' CS
 Equity Grant. Stop aspiring. Ship IL/PA/OH verified; comment out everything else with a terse note; move on.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 7. Oracle gitignored vs committed decision punted. BUG 5 oracles were gitignored because they were investigative. F4 oracle is reference documentation for gate success — commit it.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 8. Commit C dependency on Commit B is implicit. The oracle must be built from a --snapshot-all run against the NEW code (Commit B), not OLD code. State this explicitly in sequencing.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 9. Alternative framings not honestly surfaced. Steven said "query redesign," but the structurally best long-term answer is direct PDF ingestion of known state-DOE grant-report URLs + federal EIR award tables .
 Query redesign is the fast unblock; PDF ingestion is the architecturally right endgame. I should flag this as the fallback-and-follow-up, not hide it.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 What's fat and should go:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 import os, httpx…)

 ⎿   == FED EIR recipients == Q: site:ed.gov "school district" "computer science" grant recipient 2024 OR 2025

 1. Education Innovation and Research | U.S. Department of Education || http://www.ed.gov/grants-and-programs/grants-special-populations/grants-economically-disad

 2. [PDF] Fund for the Improvement of Postsecondary Education (FIPSE ... || https://www.ed.gov/media/document/frequently-asked-questions-fy-2025-fipse-sp-competition-

 … +26 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  (timeout 1m)

 ⏺ Second round destroys more of the bug memory's prescription. Let me summarize findings before entering plan mode, then write the plan.

 Empirical findings:

 1. site:*.k12.il.us / site:isbe.net = near-zero results. Districts don't self-announce grant receipts on their own sites consistently; state DOE direct-site filters are too narrow. The bug memory's primary

 prescription is wrong.

 2. What actually works (ranked):

 - PA : "awarded" "computer science" grant Pennsylvania "school district" 2025 → real hits: Berwick Area SD + PAsmart, School District of Philadelphia $450K, Saucon Valley $75K STEM, PA 2025-26 Grant Book PDF

 - OH : "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded → 3 real district-level hits (Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0)

 - IL : "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant recipients 2025 → ISBE Weekly Message PDF (real signal)

 - Fed : site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded 2025 Illinois → ed.gov EIR "Community Unit School District 60, $694,176" — a real district-level federal award

 3. Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile — my "Advancing Computer Science Education" TX guess returned 1 noise hit. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS has only 5 of 13 territory states covered and some entries are

 unverified.

 4. Truncation is secondary. With 5 queries × 13 states the real issue is low-signal corpus, not too much signal. Per-state chunked Claude calls still fix the 14K cap cleanly.

 Now entering plan mode. This plan is a one-shot — the foundational assumption has been verified against empirical Serper responses, every commit has a rollback, and call sites are enumerated.

 ⏺ Entered plan mode

 Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

 per-state chunking precedent, and /signal_funding command existence.

 ⏺ Running the ruthless pressure test. Holding the full plan in my head first, then I'll interrogate foundation, stress-test every layer, fix what's wrong, and rewrite from scratch.

 What's genuinely strong (keep):

 - Empirical foundation: 14 live Serper probes disproved the bug memory's site:*.k12.*.us prescription. That section is load-bearing truth.

 - Per-state chunked Haiku calls — clean, cheap, gives per-state log visibility.

 - Harness-first sequencing: build diagnostic tooling before touching production code.

 - Single source-of-truth helpers ( _f4_build_queries , _f4_extract_items ) callable from both harness and production.

 - Manual validation gate + separate enable commit. Correct discipline.

 - Rollback ladder + kill-switch preservation.

 What's actually wrong:

 1. STATE_DOE_NAMES missing — Type C queries are broken for ~half the territory. I wrote "<State> State Board of Education" as the template. Texas grants come from TEA (Texas Education Agency), not a "State

 Board." Ohio = ODE. California = CDE. Connecticut = CSDE. My template would miss the actual grant-issuing body for at least TX, CT, MA, and probably IN/NV. This is a silent correctness bug hiding in my

 "solution." Fix: ship a hand-curated STATE_DOE_NAMES constant covering all 13 territory states with verified proper names.

 2. Type A2 is unverified speculation. "computer science" grant "selected" OR "recipients" — I invented this, didn't probe it. Plans should not ship queries I haven't empirically tested. Cut it.

 3. Oracle bootstrap is underspecified. "Hand-curated during validation gate step" is hand-waving. Reality: somebody has to click 20-30 URLs and label them. If that's Steven, I'm wasting his time. If that's me,

 I need to describe how (httpx fetch + human review of snippets). Make explicit: I build the initial oracle via URL fetch + label proposal, Steven reviews the labels, commit the final JSON.

 4. Gate failure has no exit criterion. "Iterate on queries/prompt" is infinite. A well-run fix has a max-iteration cap. If 3 harness rounds don't get HIGH precision ≥60%, escalate to Alternative C (direct PDF

 ingestion of state-DOE grant-report PDFs + federal EIR tables). Session 57 may not close BUG 1 — that has to be OK, stated up front.

 5. Per-state JSON parse failure semantics not explicit. Current code catches JSONDecodeError at global scope and kills the whole scan on one bad response. In a 13-state loop, one bad state must not kill the

 other 12. _f4_extract_items must catch internally and return [] for the failing state. I hinted at it but didn't state it.

 6. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS aspirations waste plan bandwidth. I said "verify TX/MA during implementation." TX has no analog to PAsmart — TEA doesn't run a named district grant program comparable to Illinois' CS

 Equity Grant. Stop aspiring. Ship IL/PA/OH verified; comment out everything else with a terse note; move on.

 7. Oracle gitignored vs committed decision punted. BUG 5 oracles were gitignored because they were investigative. F4 oracle is reference documentation for gate success — commit it.

 8. Commit C dependency on Commit B is implicit. The oracle must be built from a --snapshot-all run against the NEW code (Commit B), not OLD code. State this explicitly in sequencing.

 9. Alternative framings not honestly surfaced. Steven said "query redesign," but the structurally best long-term answer is direct PDF ingestion of known state-DOE grant-report URLs + federal EIR award tables .

 Query redesign is the fast unblock; PDF ingestion is the architecturally right endgame. I should flag this as the fallback-and-follow-up, not hide it.

 What's fat and should go:

 - "Decisions" section is a numbered list restating the architecture. Fold into architecture.

 - "What this plan deliberately does NOT do" has 7 bullets — half are obvious (e.g., "no F5 changes"). Trim to the 3 non-obvious ones.

 - "Risks that aren't obvious" has 7 items; 3 of them ARE obvious (rate limits, Haiku cost). Cut.

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 BUG 1 — F4 CS Funding Scanner Query Redesign

 Session 57, 2026-04-11. One-shot plan (rev 2, post pressure-test).

 Context

 tools/signal_processor.py:scan_cs_funding_awards (line 3152) has silently returned zero extractions on every run since F4 shipped in Session 49. The Serper leg fires (Session 53 diagnostic: 456 articles across

 13 states) but Claude Haiku correctly extracts nothing because the search corpus is starving — current queries match higher-ed grants, teacher awards, student scholarships, and bill PDFs, not K-12

 district-level funding announcements. ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN has been off since Session 53. /signal_funding is manual-only (no daily schedule). Project memory: project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md .

 The bug memory's primary prescription is wrong. It said "use *.k12.*.us + state DOE subdomains." I ran 14 live Serper probes before writing this plan; that approach returns near-zero because districts don't

 self-announce grant receipts on their own websites and direct-DOE site filters over-restrict against the actually-indexed weekly-message PDFs. The empirically-winning pattern is broad natural-language queries

 + quoted state DOE proper name (no site filter) + verified program names . Evidence is preserved below; the plan is built on it.

 The approach is the fast unblock: redesign the Serper query corpus, chunk Haiku calls per-state, tighten the prompt, ship a local replay harness so iteration doesn't need Railway redeploys, and gate re-enable

 behind a ≥60%-precision oracle pass (same bar as F9). If three harness iterations still fail the gate, Session 57 does not close BUG 1 — the follow-up is Alternative C (direct ingestion of known state-DOE

 grant-report PDFs + federal EIR award tables), out of scope for this plan but flagged as the structural endgame.

 Empirical findings (2026-04-11 Serper probes — load-bearing data)

 Fourteen live queries against https://google.serper.dev/search with tbs: qdr:y .

 Dead approaches (the bug memory's prescription):

 ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Query │ Result │

 │ district" 2025 │ │

 │ "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded │ 3/5 real hits : Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0 Grant across news-herald / yahoo / ed.ohio.gov │

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant │ ISBE Weekly Message PDF with CS grant section in top 3 │

 │ recipients 2025 │ │

 │ site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded │ ed.gov EIR snippet: "Community Unit School District 60, Computer Science and Engineering and Design STEM Program, $694,176" — real │

 │ 2025 Illinois │ district + amount + purpose in the snippet │

 └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile. My TX probe ( "Advancing Computer Science Education" Texas TEA awarded districts 2025 ) returned 1 noise hit because the TX entry in STATE_CS_PROGRAMS is not a

 real program name. Program names must be verified via harness probe before entering the dict. Unverified program names burn Serper budget and add zero signal.

 Decisions baked in

 1. Six query types per state. A, C, F, G run always (state-name-parameterized). B runs only when a verified program name exists. No *.k12.*.us filters. No direct-DOE site filters.

 2. STATE_DOE_NAMES constant — 13 territory states mapped to their actual grant-issuing agency's proper name (TEA for TX, CDE for CA, ODE for OH, etc.). Generic "<State> State Board of Education" is broken for

 half the territory. This is the most important single fix vs my rev-1 draft.

 3. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS shrinks to verified-3 (IL/PA/OH). TX and MA were unverified; removed. CA/MI/CT/OK/IN/NV/TN/NE have no analog to PAsmart and don't get program-name queries. Types A/C/F/G carry these

 states.

 4. Per-state chunked Haiku extraction. One call per state, ≤20KB payload, JSONDecodeError caught per-state so one bad state can't kill the other 12. 13 calls × ~$0.002 Haiku = ~$0.02/scan (vs current 1 ×

 ~$0.01 truncated).

 5. Tightened prompt — explicit LEA-name rules, drop redundant Awards >12 months old ( qdr:y enforces it).

 6. Serper-replay harness shipped first, before any scanner changes. Uses the real _f4_build_queries and _f4_extract_items helpers — zero divergence risk.

 7. Gate is strict by default. Every HIGH-confidence extraction must be labeled in the oracle; precision = real_LEA_awards / total_labeled_HIGH ≥ 60%.

 8. Max 3 harness iterations. If the gate still fails after 3 query/prompt revisions, halt this plan and pivot to Alternative C (out of scope). Session 57 ending without closing BUG 1 is an acceptable outcome;

 shipping a scanner that produces bad data is not.

 9. ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN stays False until a separate enable commit. Commit B is dead code in production until Commit D flips the switch.

 10. Oracle is committed to git (unlike BUG 5's investigative oracles). F4 oracle is reference documentation for what "success" looked like — small file, long-lived artifact.

 Architecture

 Query generator

 # tools/signal_processor.py, next to STATE_CS_PROGRAMS (~line 2978)

 STATE_DOE_NAMES = {

 "IL" : "Illinois State Board of Education" ,

 "PA" : "Pennsylvania Department of Education" ,

 "OH" : "Ohio Department of Education" ,

 "MI" : "Michigan Department of Education" ,

 "CT" : "Connecticut State Department of Education" ,

 "OK" : "Oklahoma State Department of Education" ,

 "MA" : "Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education" ,

 "IN" : "Indiana Department of Education" ,

 "NV" : "Nevada Department of Education" ,

 "TN" : "Tennessee Department of Education" ,

 "NE" : "Nebraska Department of Education" ,

 "TX" : "Texas Education Agency" ,

 "CA" : "California Department of Education" ,

 }

 STATE_CS_PROGRAMS = {

 "IL" : [ "CS Equity Grant" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 "PA" : [ "PAsmart" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 "OH" : [ "Computer Science Promise" ], # verified 2026-04-11

 # TX ("TEA Advancing CS") and MA ("Innovation Pathways CS") were in the

 # original scanner but are unverified — probe returned noise. Types A/C/F/G

 # carry these states without program names. Do not re-add without a harness

 # probe confirming ≥3 real hits in the first 10 organic results.

 }

 def _f4_build_queries(state_abbr: str , state_name: str ) -> list [ str ]:

 """Build the F4 Serper query list for one territory state.

 Types A, C, F, G always emitted. Type B only when a verified program name

 exists. Empirically validated 2026-04-11 against live Serper — see

 project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md + Session 57 plan.

 """

 doe_name = STATE_DOE_NAMES.get(state_abbr, "" )

 queries = [

 # Type A — natural-language award announcement (highest-yield baseline)

 f'"awarded" "computer science" grant "{state_name}" "school district"' ,

 # Type F — retain the existing STEM-grant pattern (PA probe confirmed)

 f'"STEM grant" awarded school district "{state_name}"' ,

 # Type G — generic .gov filter (catches federal EIR award tables)

 f'site:.gov "{state_name}" "school district" "computer science" grant recipient' ,

 ]

 if doe_name:

 # Type C — state DOE by proper name (no site filter)

 queries.append( f'"{doe_name}" "computer science" grant recipients' )

 queries.append( f'"{doe_name}" "computer science" OR "STEM" grant awarded' )

 for program_name in STATE_CS_PROGRAMS.get(state_abbr, []):

 # Type B — verified program name

 queries.append( f'"{program_name}" awarded "{state_name}" district' )

 queries.append( f'"{program_name}" recipients "{state_name}"' )

 return queries

 For the 13-state territory with 3 verified programs:

 - Baseline per state: 3 Types (A, F, G) + 2 Type C (when DOE name set) = 5 queries

 - IL/PA/OH add 2 Type B queries each = 7 queries

 - Total: 13×5 + 3×2 = 71 queries per scan

 - Budget: 71 × $0.001 = $0.071/scan Serper + ~$0.02/scan Haiku = ~$0.09/scan

 Per-state Haiku extraction

 def _f4_extract_items(client, combined_text: str , state_abbr: str ) -> list [ dict ]:

 """Run Haiku on one state's snippets. Returns extracted items or [] on any

 failure — per-state isolation so one bad response can't kill the scan."""

 if not combined_text.strip():

 return []

 prompt = _F4_EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (combined_text=combined_text)

 try :

 response = client.messages.create(

 model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 max_tokens= 3000 ,

 messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 )

 _track_usage(response)

 raw_text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 except Exception as e:

 logger.warning( f"F4 {state_abbr}: Haiku call failed: {e}" )

 return []

 try :

 clean = raw_text

 if "```" in clean:

 clean = clean.split( "```" )[ 1 ]

 if clean.startswith( "json" ):

 clean = clean[ 4 :]

 start, end = clean.find( "[" ), clean.rfind( "]" )

 if start == - 1 or end == - 1 :

 logger.warning( f"F4 {state_abbr}: no JSON array in Haiku response" )

 return []

 return json.loads(clean[start:end + 1 ])

 except json.JSONDecodeError as e:

 logger.warning( f"F4 {state_abbr}: JSON parse failed: {e}" )

 return []

 The main scan_cs_funding_awards loop collects all_snippets per state (existing behavior preserved at lines 3184-3228 — Serper leg untouched), then replaces the global-concatenation + single-Haiku-call block

 (lines 3239-3293) with:

 import anthropic

 client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 90.0 )

 items = []

 for state_abbr in scan_states:

 state_snippets = [s for s in all_snippets if s[ "searched_state" ] == state_abbr]

 if not state_snippets:

 continue

 combined_text = ""

 for s in state_snippets:

 combined_text += f"\n[{state_abbr}] {s['title']}\n{s['snippet']}\nURL: {s['url']}\n"

 combined_text = combined_text[: 20000 ]

 state_items = _f4_extract_items(client, combined_text, state_abbr)

 logger.info( f"F4 {state_abbr}: { len (state_snippets)} snippets → { len (state_items)} extracted" )

 items.extend(state_items)

 The downstream routing loop (current lines 3322-3415: dedup → NCES validation → HIGH→queue, MED/LOW→signals, active→customer_intel) is unchanged . This plan only touches the Serper query leg and the Haiku

 extraction leg.

 Extract the prompt into _F4_EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE module constant (private). Two substantive changes vs current:

 1. New DISTRICT NAME RULES block (before EXCLUDE: ):

 DISTRICT NAME RULES:

 - Must be a specific named Local Education Agency (LEA) — the exact

 legal name of one K-12 school district.

 - REJECT program descriptions (e.g. "23 west central Illinois schools")

 - REJECT aggregate phrases ("CS grant recipients", "participating districts")

 - REJECT charter networks, CMOs, ESCs, BOCES unless the snippet clearly

 names a single LEA as the direct recipient

 - Single school without district context → confidence=LOW, mark for review

 2. Delete the - Awards >12 months old exclude line (redundant with qdr:y ).

 Everything else in the prompt stays byte-identical (including source_url rules — the Session 53 fix for hallucinated URLs).

 Commit A — Serper-replay diagnostic harness

 New file: scripts/f4_serper_replay.py (pattern mirrors scripts/bug5_dryrun.py ).

 Modes:

 - --snapshot <state> — runs _f4_build_queries for one state against live Serper, saves scripts/f4_snapshots/<state>_<YYYYMMDD>.json with every organic result's {title, snippet, url, searched_state} .

 - --snapshot-all — runs all 13 territory states sequentially (honors existing 0.5s Serper throttle).

 - --replay <snapshot_path> — loads a snapshot, calls _f4_extract_items offline, prints the extracted JSON list plus per-state counts.

 - --gate <oracle_path> <snapshot_dir> — loads the oracle, replays extraction across all snapshots in the directory, computes HIGH-confidence precision = (HIGH items matching oracle is_real_lea_award=true ) /

 (total labeled HIGH items). Exit 0 if ≥60%; exit 1 if below or if any HIGH extraction is unlabeled.

 Critical: the harness import s _f4_build_queries and _f4_extract_items from tools.signal_processor — no duplication, no divergence path.

 Rollback: git revert — zero production impact either way.

 Commit B — scanner redesign

 tools/signal_processor.py only:

 1. Line 2978 — replace STATE_CS_PROGRAMS with the verified-3 subset; add STATE_DOE_NAMES constant above it.

 2. Private helpers added next to the constants: _f4_build_queries , _f4_extract_items , _F4_EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE .

 3. Lines 3194-3205 — replace inline 5-query list with _f4_build_queries(state_abbr, state_name) .

 4. Lines 3239-3293 — replace global-concat + single-Haiku block with the per-state loop shown in Architecture.

 5. Preserved : lines 3322-3415 routing logic (HIGH→queue, MED/LOW→signals, active→intel), dedup, NCES validation, _track_usage , error logging around the outer function body.

 Kill switch stays False . Commit B is dead code in production. Verified by pre-flight check #2.

 Rollback: git revert — single file, no cross-module coupling.

 Commit C — oracle + gate pass

 Oracle build process (explicit because rev-1 hand-waved this):

 1. Run python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --snapshot-all . Produces 13 JSON snapshots.

 2. Run python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --replay scripts/f4_snapshots/<state>_<date>.json for each of IL/PA/OH/CA/TX (5 highest-priority states). Produces extracted-item lists.

 3. I build an initial oracle proposal by iterating the union of extracted items, fetching each source_url via httpx , reading the page content, and labeling is_real_lea_award: true/false/null based on whether

 the page actually documents a K-12 LEA receiving a CS grant. Target: 20-30 labeled rows across the 5 states.

 4. Commit the oracle as scripts/f4_oracle.json . Each row: {district, state, source_url, expected_confidence, is_real_lea_award, notes} . null = "could not verify" (excluded from precision calc).

 5. Steven reviews the proposed labels — this is ~5 minutes of spot-checking, not 20 minutes of first-pass labeling. Any label he flips, I re-commit.

 6. Run python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --gate scripts/f4_oracle.json scripts/f4_snapshots/ — expect PASS with precision ≥60%.

 If gate fails: iterate _f4_build_queries or _F4_EXTRACTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE in a follow-up commit on top of B, re-run harness. Hard cap: 3 iteration rounds. After round 3, halt BUG 1 at Commit B (dead code,

 kill switch off) and escalate to Alternative C in a later session.

 Rollback: git revert of oracle — no production impact.

 Commit D — enable flip

 After Commit C gate passes:

 # tools/signal_processor.py line 1128

 -ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN = False # F4: DISABLED Session 53 …

 +ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN = True # F4: re-enabled Session 57 after BUG 1 query redesign

 Push. Wait for Railway "Scout is online." Live smoke test via Telethon:

 python3 scripts/tg_send.py "/signal_funding"

 python3 scripts/tg_recent.py -- limit 20

 Pass criteria: ≥5 signals written across 13 states OR ≥1 HIGH-confidence auto-queue. Zero would mean the gate was mis-calibrated → flip back to False , investigate.

 Rollback: one-line flip True → False , push. No sheet cleanup unless the live run actually wrote contaminated rows (unlikely for a first bad run at this scan size).

 Verification

 Pre-flight (before pushing Commit B)

 source .venv/bin/activate

 python3 -c "

 from tools.signal_processor import (

 scan_cs_funding_awards, STATE_CS_PROGRAMS, STATE_DOE_NAMES,

 _f4_build_queries, _f4_extract_items, ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN, TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA,

 )

 assert ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN is False, 'kill switch must stay off until Commit D'

 assert set(STATE_CS_PROGRAMS.keys()) == {'IL','PA','OH'}

 assert set(STATE_DOE_NAMES.keys()) == set(TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA), 'DOE map must cover all 13 territory states'

 assert STATE_DOE_NAMES['TX'] == 'Texas Education Agency' # not 'Texas State Board of Education'

 assert STATE_DOE_NAMES['CA'] == 'California Department of Education'

 assert len(_f4_build_queries('IL','Illinois')) == 7 # 3 baseline + 2 Type C + 2 Type B

 assert len(_f4_build_queries('CA','California')) == 5 # 3 baseline + 2 Type C, no Type B

 # Kill-switch short-circuit

 r = scan_cs_funding_awards()

 assert r == {'signals': [], 'queued': [], 'customer_intel': [], 'raw_count': 0}

 print('pre-flight PASS')

 "

 Harness (Commit A deliverable → used for Commit C)

 python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --snapshot-all

 ls scripts/f4_snapshots/

 # Expect 13 JSON files, one per territory state

 python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --replay scripts/f4_snapshots/PA_20260411.json

 # Expect: real district extractions including Berwick Area School District (from the 2026-04-11 probe)

 python3 scripts/f4_serper_replay.py --gate scripts/f4_oracle.json scripts/f4_snapshots/

 # Expect: PASS, precision ≥60%

 Live smoke test (Commit D)

 python3 scripts/tg_send.py "/signal_funding"

 # Wait ~3 min for 71 Serper calls + 13 Haiku calls

 python3 scripts/tg_recent.py -- limit 20

 # Expect: "💵 CS Funding Scan — Raw extracted: ≥5, Signals written: ≥5"

 Railway log check (via existing RAILWAY_TOKEN pattern):

 F4 IL: 42 snippets → 3 extracted

 F4 PA: 51 snippets → 4 extracted

 F4 OH: 38 snippets → 2 extracted

 F4 TX: 29 snippets → 0 extracted (acceptable — no verified program name)

 Sheet check against the Signals tab (filter Signal Type == cs_funding_award ) — expect ≥5 rows dated today.

 1. Oracle rots on qdr:y rotation. Six months from now the 1-year window has shifted and the oracle references articles Serper no longer returns. Mitigation: the oracle is a point-in-time artifact; regeneration

 cost is ~20 minutes and only needed when re-verifying F4. Document in the oracle file's own comment.

 2. Per-state JSON parse failure semantics. Explicitly handled in _f4_extract_items — one bad state logs and returns [] , other 12 proceed. Pre-flight test should include a synthetic broken-JSON case but that's

 tested inline in the harness, not as a separate commit.

 3. Prompt false-negatives from tighter LEA rule. New "specific named LEA" rule will reject edge cases like "participating districts include…" where the list lives in a linked PDF. This is preferred over the

 Session 49-56 baseline of 0 extractions — any non-fabricated output is a net improvement, and MED/LOW still routes to Signals for human review.

 4. Gate failure is a real outcome. Max 3 iterations, then halt. BUG 1 may not close this session. Stated up front so we don't rationalize mid-failure.

 Alternative C — the structural endgame (out of scope, documented as follow-up)

 If the query-redesign gate fails three times, or even if it passes but yields <5 HIGH signals/scan, the right long-term move is direct ingestion of known state-DOE grant-report PDFs + federal EIR award tables .

 Maintain a small curated list of ~20 URLs (PA Grant Book, ISBE Weekly Message archive, ed.gov EIR FY20XX awards page, NSF CS for All CRDC lookup, etc.), download them quarterly, Claude-extract district

 recipients. Higher-density signal, lower false-positive rate, and doesn't depend on Google ranking. This would be a separate plan and a different shape of scanner, so it's out of scope for BUG 1 — but the

 harness in Commit A should support --replay from PDF-extracted text as well as Serper snapshots so the Alternative C harness reuses the same gate machinery.

 Zero agent/main.py changes, zero schema changes, zero sheets_writer / district_prospector signature changes.

 Out of scope (load-bearing three only)

 - No scheduled scan wiring. /signal_funding stays manual. Daily schedule addition is a separate Session-58+ decision conditional on a week of manual runs confirming stable HIGH precision.

 - No Alternative C PDF ingestion. Follow-up if the query-redesign gate fails or yield is too low.

 - No customer_intel routing changes. Current behavior: district match to active customer → logs as intel, does not queue. A real upward opp at a partially-customer district may be misrouted; that's a separate

 strategic decision, unrelated to this bug's root cause.

 Rollback ladder

 1. Commit A — git revert ; harness is read-only, zero production impact either way.

 2. Commit B — git revert OR leave in place (kill switch keeps it dead code).

 3. Commit C — git revert of the oracle; no production impact.

 4. Commit D — one-line flip True → False , push. Done. If the live run wrote rows to the Signals tab before rollback, leave them — operator reviews via /signals and acts manually. Scanner has never queued from

 F4 historically, so the first bad run is unlikely to be large.

 ---

 Most important changes vs rev-1 (reasoning preserved)

 1. Added STATE_DOE_NAMES constant covering all 13 territory states. Rev-1 used "<State> State Board of Education" as a generic template, which is broken for TX (TEA, not SBOE), CA (CDE), OH (ODE), CT (CSDE),

 MA (DESE), and several others. This was a silent correctness bug hiding inside the "solution." Verified agency names are well-known facts, no probing required.

 2. Dropped unverified Type A2 query ( "computer science" grant "selected" OR "recipients" ). I invented this in rev-1 and didn't probe it. Plans should not ship queries I haven't empirically tested.

 3. Shrunk STATE_CS_PROGRAMS to IL/PA/OH only. Rev-1 aspired to verify TX/MA "during implementation." Reality: TX has no analog to PAsmart (TEA doesn't run a named district-facing grant program comparable to

 IL's CS Equity Grant). Stop aspiring. Ship the verified 3, carry the other 10 states with Types A/C/F/G.

 4. Made oracle build explicit. Rev-1 said "hand-curated during validation gate step" which hand-waved who does what. Rev-2: I fetch each candidate URL, propose labels, Steven spot-reviews — ~5 minutes of his

 time, not 20.

 5. Explicit max-3-iteration cap on gate failure. Rev-1's "iterate" was infinite. Rev-2 halts at 3 rounds and escalates to Alternative C. Session 57 ending without closing BUG 1 is an acceptable outcome;

 shipping a broken scanner is not.

 6. JSONDecodeError handled per-state inside _f4_extract_items . Rev-1 hinted at this; rev-2 makes it explicit. One bad state returns [] and logs; the other 12 proceed.

 7. Oracle committed to git (not gitignored like BUG 5's oracles). F4 oracle is reference documentation for what success looked like — long-lived artifact.

 8. Alternative C (PDF ingestion) flagged as the structural endgame. Rev-1 hid this. Rev-2 surfaces it honestly: query redesign is the fast unblock; direct PDF ingestion of known DOE grant reports + federal EIR

 tables is architecturally sounder long-term. Harness designed so Alternative C can reuse the same gate machinery.

 9. Trimmed meta-commentary. Rev-1 had 7-bullet "does NOT do" + 7-item risk list + separate "Decisions" section + duplicated pseudocode+prose. Rev-2 folds decisions into architecture, cuts risks to 4

 load-bearing items, scope to 3 load-bearing items, keeps one pseudocode per helper. ~80 lines shorter without losing content.

 ⏺ Second round destroys more of the bug memory's prescription. Let me summarize findings before entering plan mode, then write the plan.

 Empirical findings:

 1. site:*.k12.il.us / site:isbe.net = near-zero results. Districts don't self-announce grant receipts on their own sites consistently; state DOE direct-site filters are too narrow. The bug memory's primary

 prescription is wrong.

 2. What actually works (ranked):

 - PA : "awarded" "computer science" grant Pennsylvania "school district" 2025 → real hits: Berwick Area SD + PAsmart, School District of Philadelphia $450K, Saucon Valley $75K STEM, PA 2025-26 Grant Book PDF

 - OH : "Computer Science Promise" Ohio district awarded → 3 real district-level hits (Educational Service Center Teach CS 2.0)

 - IL : "Illinois State Board of Education" "computer science" grant recipients 2025 → ISBE Weekly Message PDF (real signal)

 - Fed : site:.gov "computer science" grant "school district" awarded 2025 Illinois → ed.gov EIR "Community Unit School District 60, $694,176" — a real district-level federal award

 3. Program-name queries are high-signal BUT fragile — my "Advancing Computer Science Education" TX guess returned 1 noise hit. STATE_CS_PROGRAMS has only 5 of 13 territory states covered and some entries are

 unverified.

 4. Truncation is secondary. With 5 queries × 13 states the real issue is low-signal corpus, not too much signal. Per-state chunked Claude calls still fix the 14K cap cleanly.

 Now entering plan mode. This plan is a one-shot — the foundational assumption has been verified against empirical Serper responses, every commit has a rollback, and call sites are enumerated.
